name: sast-bandit
description: Python security vulnerability detection using Bandit SAST with CWE and OWASP mapping. Use when: (1) Scanning Python code for security vulnerabilities and anti-patterns, (2) Identifying hardcoded secrets, SQL injection, command injection, and insecure APIs, (3) Generating security reports with severity classifications for CI/CD pipelines, (4) Providing remediation guidance with security framework references, (5) Enforcing Python security best practices in development workflows.
capabilities: Scan Python files and directories for security vulnerabilities using Bandit
Generate security reports with severity and confidence classifications
Map findings to CWE identifiers and OWASP Top 10 categories
Provide remediation guidance with secure code examples
Configure pre-commit hooks for git workflow integration
Integrate security scanning into CI/CD pipelines
author: AgentSecOps
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing YAML configs and Markdown guides for Bandit SAST tool. All 257 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the skill shows VULNERABLE code patterns as examples of what Bandit DETECTS, not actual malicious code. No executable scripts found. Purely defensive security documentation.
valueStatement: Python code often contains hidden security risks like hardcoded passwords, SQL injection flaws, and command injection vulnerabilities. This skill integrates Bandit SAST scanning to automatically detect security issues in your Python codebase and maps findings to industry security frameworks.
userTitle: Scan Python Code for Security Vulnerabilities
limitations: Only scans Python code; other languages not supported
Detects known patterns; may miss business logic vulnerabilities
Results may include false positives requiring manual review
Cannot detect vulnerabilities in compiled dependencies

useCases.title: Automated Security Gates
useCases.description: Integrate Bandit scanning into CI/CD pipelines to block deployments with high severity vulnerabilities.

useCases.title: Pre-commit Security Checks
useCases.description: Add pre-commit hooks that scan Python files before commits to catch security issues early.

useCases.title: Vulnerability Assessment
useCases.description: Scan legacy Python codebases to identify and prioritize security remediation efforts.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Security Scan
promptTemplates.scenario: Scan a Python file for issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan the Python file [FILE_PATH] for security vulnerabilities using the sast-bandit skill. Report all findings with severity, confidence, and CWE references.

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that uses Bandit to scan Python code on pull requests. Block merges if high severity issues are found.

promptTemplates.title: Remediation Guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: A Bandit scan found possible SQL injection (B608) in [FILE_PATH]. Provide remediation guidance with secure code examples using parameterized queries.

promptTemplates.title: Pre-commit Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a pre-commit configuration that runs Bandit to prevent commits with high severity security issues.

bestPractices: Run Bandit scans on every commit using pre-commit hooks to catch issues early
Integrate scanning into CI/CD pipelines with severity gates that block high-risk findings
Document false positive suppressions with inline # nosec comments explaining the justification
antiPatterns: Skipping security scans to speed up development cycles
Ignoring low-confidence findings without manual review
Committing .env files with real credentials to version control

faq.question: What Python versions does Bandit support?
faq.answer: Bandit supports Python 3.8 and later versions. The skill requires Python >=3.8 for full functionality.

faq.question: How long does a typical scan take?
faq.answer: Scan time depends on codebase size. Bandit parallelizes scans automatically. A typical 1000-line project scans in under 10 seconds.

faq.question: Can I integrate Bandit with my existing CI/CD tool?
faq.answer: Yes, Bandit works with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI, and other CI platforms. Config examples are included in the skill.

faq.question: Does this skill access or store my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides documentation and configuration. Bandit runs locally on your machine and does not transmit code externally.

faq.question: Why am I seeing false positives?
faq.answer: Bandit uses pattern matching and may flag legitimate code. Use confidence filtering (-i flag) or inline # nosec comments to suppress known false positives.

faq.question: How does this compare to other Python security tools?
faq.answer: Bandit focuses on Python-specific vulnerabilities like pickle deserialization and hardcoded secrets. Combine with Semgrep for broader coverage and Safety for dependency scanning.
